gateRules.trigger: salmon smoking, smoke house, smoking temperatures, food storage, dried salmon, salt-cured
gateRules.action: GATE -- food safety: salmon must reach minimum internal temperature of 160°F (71°C) during hot smoking. Cold smoking (below 90°F) does not kill pathogens — cold-smoked products require either salt curing first or refrigerated storage. Inspect for spoilage before consuming stored smoked salmon: off odor, slimy texture, or visible mold are discard signals.

gateRules.trigger: storing smoked salmon, dried salmon storage, fish preservation storage
gateRules.action: GATE -- storage safety: properly smoked and dried salmon stored in a cool, dry location with air circulation lasts 1-2 weeks at room temperature, several months if vacuum sealed and refrigerated. Do not store in airtight containers without drying fully first (moisture enables mold). Botulism is a risk in improperly sealed vacuum-packed fish.

gateRules.description: Marine GATE — reef net fishing on water
gateRules.trigger: reef net, canoe fishing, paddling, water activity, reef net station
gateRules.action: GATE -- marine safety: reef net fishing requires two anchored canoes on the water. PFD required for all persons on or near water. Buddy system mandatory. Cold water shock: 1-10-1 rule (1 minute to control breathing, 10 minutes of meaningful movement, 1 hour before unconsciousness in cold water). Check tidal conditions and weather before deployment.

gateRules.description: Marine GATE — cold water immersion response
gateRules.trigger: capsizing, falling from canoe, water immersion during fishing
gateRules.action: GATE -- cold water response: 1-10-1 rule applies. Do not remove clothing. Stay with the vessel. Use HELP position. Signal for assistance immediately.

annotateRules.trigger: filleting salmon, knife work, salmon processing
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- knife safety: use a sharp, flexible filleting knife. Cut away from your body. Stabilize the fish on a non-slip surface. Keep fingers clear of the knife path. A sharp knife requires less force and is safer than a dull one.

annotateRules.trigger: stake driving, weir construction, woven branch
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- tool safety: driving stakes requires a maul or mallet; wear gloves to prevent blisters and splinters; inspect stakes for splits before driving.

safetyNotes.foodSafety: Salmon must reach minimum internal temperature of 160°F (71°C) during hot smoking. Cold smoking does not kill pathogens and requires additional curing or refrigeration. Botulism risk in improperly sealed vacuum-packed fish is real and produces no visible, odor, or taste warning.
safetyNotes.toolSafety: Sharp filleting knives require proper grip, stable cutting surface, and cut direction away from body. Weir stake driving requires gloves and mallet inspection.
safetyNotes.marineSafety: Reef net fishing from canoes carries cold water immersion risk. PFD required, buddy system mandatory, 1-10-1 cold water rule applies, tidal and weather conditions must be checked before any water activity.
